steps.title: Use the hypothesis
steps.content: The sentence proposes that helicopter surveying may lead to overestimates of cougar population density. A useful example must therefore connect helicopter surveying with a density result that looks unusually high compared with results from other studies, not merely compare study locations or study sizes.

steps.title: Check the evidence
steps.content: Choice A compares the size of two study areas, which does not show an overestimate of density. Choice C reports a low maximum density, so it points in the opposite direction. Choice D compares Davidson et al. with a camera-trapping study but does not establish a helicopter-related overestimate. Choice B gives Choate et al.'s maximum density of 10.24 individuals per 100 square kilometers, higher than several other studies, so it fits the proposed explanation. The answer is B.
